reason: Step (ii) writes ||(R_F - g_N(S_F^2)) j|| <= ||R_F - g_N(S_F^2)|| ||j||_op and attributes the composition bound to GT-opnorm. But (R_F - g_N(S_F^2)) is an ELEMENT a of the Banach algebra A, and 'a j' = (left-multiplication by a) composed with j. ||R_F - g_N(S_F^2)|| (=eps_N^F) is the ELEMENT/algebra norm, whereas GT-opnorm's bound ||T j||<=||T||_op ||j||_op is for an OPERATOR T with its OPERATOR norm. The actual bound needed is ||a j||_op <= ||a||_element ||j||_op, which is Banach-algebra SUBMULTIPLICATIVITY (||a x|| <= ||a|| ||x||, an axiom of the unital Banach algebra A per H1), NOT the GT-opnorm operator-composition fact. The distinction is non-vacuous: Kitaev himself (:642+) only gets ||Lambda_X|| <= (1+eps)||X|| in the approximate setting. Here, in an EXACT Banach algebra (H1), the constant is 1 and eps_N^F -> 0 either way, so the LIMIT CONCLUSION R_F j = j R_Phi is ROBUST and correct. But the justification should cite submultiplicativity of A's (and B's) norm (H1), not GT-opnorm, for the a-j and j-U bounds. Non-blocking but fix the citation. (Same convergence is in the ELEMENT norm of A/B, which is exactly what GT-taylor-calculus delivers - that part is fine.)
